help.hint_1: Create folders before putting files inside them.
help.hint_2: When you are unsure, inspect the folder first, then make the next small change.

Do not use sudo in this beginner workspace.

copy-with-cp.title: Use `cp` when you need a backup
copy-with-cp.bodyMarkdown:
`cp` copies a file. The original stays where it was.

```bash
cp agenda.txt agenda-backup.txt
```

Use `cp` when the original file should remain available.

move-with-mv.title: Use `mv` to move a file
move-with-mv.bodyMarkdown:
`mv` moves a file into another folder.

```bash
mv agenda.txt handoff/agenda.txt
```

After a move, the file is no longer in the old location.

rename-with-mv.title: Use `mv` to rename a file
rename-with-mv.bodyMarkdown:
`mv` can also rename a file when the destination is a new name in the same folder.

```bash
mv draft.txt final.txt
```

Think of rename as moving the file to a new name.
